questions.question: What is the primary goal of cloud computing?
options: A) To provide on-demand computing resources
questions.explanation: The primary goal of cloud computing is to provide on-demand computing resources, which can be scaled up or down based on the needs of the user.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a distributed system?
options: A) All nodes are connected in a single network
B) All nodes are independent and can operate separately
C) All nodes are identical and have the same capabilities
D) All nodes are located in the same physical location
questions.explanation: A characteristic of a distributed system is that all nodes are independent and can operate separately, allowing for greater flexibility and scalability.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a cloud computing model?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are examples of cloud computing models. Public clouds provide on-demand computing resources to multiple users, private clouds provide on-demand computing resources to a single organization, and hybrid clouds combine public and private clouds.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using a cloud-based infrastructure?
questions.explanation: The primary benefit of using a cloud-based infrastructure is that it reduces upfront costs, as users only pay for the resources they use, rather than investing in and maintaining their own infrastructure.

questions.question: Which of the following security measures is commonly used in cloud computing?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are commonly used security measures in cloud computing. Encryption is used to protect data in transit and at rest, firewalls are used to block unauthorized access to the cloud, and intrusion detection systems are used to detect and respond to security threats.
